meta.title: Research Portal — Work Flow Map
meta.subtitle: How data and work move through the 4-layer pipeline (Ingestion → Storage → Views → Agents)
meta.vision_note: Lanes follow the framing from the vision doc — a new source should be a new adapter into Ingestion, not a UI rewrite.

lanes.label: Ingestion
lanes.blurb: Sources that bring new data or user intent in

lanes.label: Views
lanes.blurb: UI screens that read from storage

flows.name: Gmail research ingest
flows.trigger: User runs the research-ingest CLI when new [Research]-tagged emails accrue

steps.label: Gmail [Research] inbox
steps.detail: ylresearchwiki@gmail.com via OAuth (scripts/credentials.json + token.json). PDFs extracted with PyMuPDF; HTML bodies cleaned with BeautifulSoup.

steps.label: kb_articles row
steps.detail: Title, source_url, content, date, category. PDF attachment text inlined. PDFs cached to scripts/pdfs/.

steps.label: Analyze new
steps.detail: Generates summary, key_takeaways, themes, questions, investment_implications + Portfolio exposure: TICKERS line. Published_date pulled from source, not email date.

steps.label: kb_articles (enriched)
steps.detail: Same row, analysis columns populated.

steps.label: Obsidian export
steps.detail: Writes one markdown file per article to C:/Users/ylin1/obsidianvault/Research Wiki/{category}/{title}.md with frontmatter.

steps.label: Knowledge Base + Dashboard feed
steps.detail: Dashboard.jsx pulls latest 5 ordered by created_at; KnowledgeBase.jsx shows the full list and supports delete.

flows.name: Add companies (batch insert)
flows.trigger: User writes a .cjs insert script and runs `node insert-batchN.cjs`

steps.label: .cjs insert script
steps.detail: Pattern: dotenv + @supabase/supabase-js, upserts the company row plus a row per field. ID convention: `companyname_s`.

steps.label: companies + company_fields
steps.detail: companies: name, sector, sub_sector, priority, is_public. company_fields: one row per (company_id, field_key) — overview / products / customers / industry / competitive / transactions. Financials field intentionally left empty.

steps.label: Sector pages + GenericReview
steps.detail: App.jsx groups by sector → opens GenericReview.jsx for non-bespoke tickers (CoreWeave / Oracle / Meta / etc. have hand-built pages).

flows.name: Inline field edit (auto-save)
flows.trigger: User types in any of the 6 structured fields on a company page

steps.label: Keystroke in GenericReview
steps.detail: useAutoSave debounces 700ms after last keystroke, then fires upsertField.

steps.label: company_fields upsert
steps.detail: Single row replaced per (company_id, field_key). edited_at updates — this is what drives the Recent Updates feed.

steps.label: Dashboard · Recent Updates
steps.detail: Home page lists most-recently-edited companies, priority-filterable (High / Medium / Low).

steps.label: Field coverage audit
steps.detail: Flags companies with mostly-empty fields. Audit results surface in the AuditLog tab.

flows.name: Deep Dive (Knowledge / Interests)
flows.trigger: User adds a concept or deep-dive entry via a .cjs update script
flows.frequency: Ad-hoc, when a topic warrants a long-form take

steps.label: add_deep_dive / update_concept scripts
steps.detail: Concept-first scripts seed or rewrite a topic; deep-dive scripts attach the long-form analysis.

steps.label: concepts + deep_dives
steps.detail: Two tables: concepts (short definitions / glossary) and deep_dives (long-form linked to concept).

steps.label: Knowledge / Interests tab
steps.detail: User convention: 'Deep Dive' always means this tab in the portal.

steps.label: Verify + dump scripts
steps.detail: Validation + export-for-review utilities.

flows.name: Live financials fetch
flows.trigger: User opens the Financials tab on a company review

steps.label: Open Financials tab
steps.detail: Frontend hits /api/financials with ticker + JWT auth header (session-gated).

steps.label: /api/financials function
steps.detail: Pulls SEC EDGAR XBRL company facts (CIK-mapped for MU, ORCL, NVDA, AMZN, MSFT, GOOGL, TSLA, LITE, COHR, APLD, CIFR, WULF, CRWV) + Yahoo Finance for live price / PE.

steps.label: financials_cache
steps.detail: Cached response to avoid hammering EDGAR. Manual snapshots intentionally avoided — page always shows live or near-live.

steps.label: FinancialsTab render
steps.detail: Statements, ratios, price chart.

steps.label: Cron trigger
steps.detail: vercel.json `crons` block hits /api/cron-compute-pricing with CRON_SECRET.

steps.label: Pull AWS / Azure / GCP / Neoclouds
steps.detail: Vantage (AWS p*/g*/trn/inf/dl families), Azure Retail Prices API, GCP Cloud Billing Catalog; classifies SKUs via COHORT_MAP.

steps.label: compute_prices
steps.detail: Upserts by (sku, date). Service-role key bypasses RLS for writes.

steps.label: Digital Infra / AI tabs
steps.detail: Cohort-average pricing trends rendered with Recharts.

steps.label: Run audit stages
steps.detail: 01 field_coverage · 02 labels_and_classification · 03 freshness · 04 industry_research_hardcoded · 05 knowledge_base · 06 agents_and_pipeline · 07 cross_checks · 08 accuracy_spot_check · 09 report

steps.label: Audit scripts read Supabase + repo
steps.detail: Each stage writes findings to scripts/audit/results/.

steps.label: agent_runs (logged)
steps.detail: One row per run; supports a historical audit trail.

steps.label: AuditLog tab
steps.detail: Surfaces latest findings + run history.

flows.name: Sell-side research aggregation
flows.trigger: User runs the @sellside skill for a company

steps.label: @sellside · 8 brokers in parallel
steps.detail: GS, MS, JPM, Barclays, BofA, DB, Citi, WF. Auto-spawns @sellside-login if any broker is unauthed.

steps.label: Filesystem — ~/Claude/Sellside/<Company>/
steps.detail: Currently PDFs only. No portal-side landing table yet.

steps.label: (planned) Per-company sell-side section
steps.detail: Per vision doc: adapter writes broker reports into a documents table; company page renders them with broker / date filter.

steps.label: (planned) Summarize + tag
steps.detail: Mirror analyze_new.py pattern — summary, key_takeaways, attribution.

flows.name: Third-party research aggregation
flows.trigger: User runs the @third-party-research agent for a company

steps.label: @third-party-research · 4 sources in parallel
steps.detail: Reorg, 9fin, CreditSights, Third Bridge.

steps.label: (planned) documents table
steps.detail: Per vision: one normalized documents table — source_type, company link, date, content.

steps.label: (planned) Third-party tab
steps.detail: Aggregated coverage view with source filter on each company page.

steps.label: (planned) Same analyze pattern
steps.detail: Reuse analyze_new.py logic over this content.

flows.name: In-app content trackers (6 tabs, one pattern)
flows.trigger: User CRUDs a row in Prompts / Principles / Sources / Quick Notes / Goals / Agents tabs

steps.label: In-tab edit (1s debounce) + optional CLI seed
steps.detail: Prompts: save_prompt / save_prompt2 / save_prompt3.py seed the company-analysis template, plus inline edit. Principles & Goals: inline CRUD + drag-reorder (sort_order). Sources: import_sources.py seeds ~25 sources by category (news / research / sellside / filings) + inline add/delete. Quick Notes: insert-only, no inline edit. Agents: seed-agents.cjs populates agent_definitions; log-agent-run.cjs writes a run row from any caller (audits, etc.).

steps.label: Per-tracker tables
steps.file: Supabase · prompts / principles / sources / quick_notes / goals / agent_definitions + agent_runs
steps.detail: Each tracker owns one table (Agents owns two: definitions + runs). All RLS-gated; writes need a valid session JWT.

steps.label: One .jsx per tracker
steps.file: src/Prompts.jsx · Principles.jsx · Sources.jsx · QuickNotes.jsx · Goals.jsx · AgentsTools.jsx
steps.detail: Each tab reads its table on mount, renders editable rows, and persists changes back. agent_runs also surfaces in the AuditLog tab.

flows.name: Bespoke company reviews (no-DB)
flows.trigger: User edits a hand-rolled src/<Company>Review.jsx file in their IDE
flows.frequency: Ad-hoc · per company event (earnings, deal, news)

steps.label: Edit JSX directly
steps.file: src/CoreweaveReview.jsx · AppliedDigitalReview · CipherDigitalReview · TerawulfReview · MicronReview · OracleReview · MetaReview · TractCapitalReview
steps.detail: 8 hand-rolled review pages. CoreWeave is 1,241 lines. Vision doc explicitly defers the CoreWeave refactor — called out as a heavy lift to be done in iterations, not next-priority.

steps.label: (none — content IS the source)
steps.detail: No Supabase rows. The source files ARE the storage. Implication: edits can't be made from inside the deployed app — only via IDE → push.

steps.label: Rich per-company pages
steps.detail: App.jsx routes specific tickers to their bespoke review; everything else falls through to GenericReview.jsx (the schema-driven path).

steps.label: git push → Vercel auto-deploy
steps.detail: Vercel project `research-portal` auto-deploys on push to main. verify-deploy.mjs confirms the deploy.

flows.name: Static reference tabs (no-DB)
flows.trigger: User edits a reference src/<Tab>.jsx file in their IDE
flows.frequency: Ad-hoc · when reference material needs updating

steps.label: Edit JSX directly
steps.file: src/Primer.jsx · BusinessModels.jsx · Accounting.jsx · CreditInstruments.jsx · Restructuring.jsx · CaseStudies.jsx · IndustryResearch.jsx · AIDisruption.jsx · ProductPrimer.jsx · PrimerNewTabs.jsx · PrimerNewTabs2.jsx
steps.detail: Pure JSX with hardcoded content — no Supabase reads. Same edit-and-push lifecycle as bespoke reviews, but content is general-purpose reference (industry primers, accounting, restructuring), not per-company.

steps.label: (none — content IS the source)
steps.detail: Same constraint as bespoke reviews: cannot be edited from inside the app.

steps.label: Reference tabs
steps.detail: Mostly read-only education / reference. Primer / PrimerNewTabs / PrimerNewTabs2 is the iteration history of the industry primer.

steps.label: git push → Vercel auto-deploy
steps.detail: Same deploy path.

flows.name: Maintenance / one-off scripts
flows.trigger: User runs a fix-up or migration script manually when something needs patching

steps.label: Run a maintenance script
steps.detail: fix_portfolio_line.cjs · rewrite_analysis.py · write_analysis.py · portfolio_implications.py · migrate-hex-to-tokens.cjs · check_inference_terms.cjs · dry-run-pricing-cron.mjs · trial-pricing-apis.mjs · verify-compute-prices.mjs · verify-deploy.mjs.

steps.label: Script patches DB or runs a smoke test
steps.detail: fix_portfolio_line patches kb_articles so 'Portfolio exposure:' sits on its own line. rewrite_analysis bulk-updates investment_implications. portfolio_implications keyword-matches against PORTFOLIO_TICKERS to set portfolio exposure. migrate-hex-to-tokens was a one-shot theme migration. verify-compute-prices / trial-pricing-apis / dry-run-pricing-cron are read-only smoke tests for the weekly compute-pricing cron. verify-deploy.mjs confirms the Vercel deployment.

steps.label: Patches to existing tables
steps.detail: Most fix-ups target kb_articles. Some hit compute_prices for verification (read-only).

steps.label: Re-render with fix
steps.detail: No dedicated view — same tabs as the underlying data flow.
